# Bangkok city guide

## Overview

Bangkok (กรุงเทพ) is sensory overload in the best possible way. Golden temples, chaotic street food markets, rooftop bars with skyline views, and tuk-tuks weaving through traffic that never seems to move. It's hot, humid, and endlessly energizing. Use Bangkok as a hub for exploring Chiang Mai, Phuket, Krabi, and Phang Nga.

**Best time to visit**: Nov-Feb for cooler, drier weather. Avoid Apr-May (scorching). Jun-Oct is rainy season but cheaper.
**Currency**: Thai Baht (THB). 35 THB ≈ 1 USD.
**Language tip**: Learn "sawadee kha/khrap" (hello) and "khop khun kha/khrap" (thank you). Add "kha" (women) or "khrap" (men) for politeness.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Khao San Road | Backpacker, chaotic | Budget stays, street food, nightlife | Taxi/tuk-tuk from Phra Athit pier |
| Sukhumvit | Modern, expat | Fine dining, rooftop bars, malls | BTS Asok, Phrom Phong, Thong Lo |
| Silom/Sathorn | Business, nightlife | Patpong Market, sky bars, temples | BTS Sala Daeng, MRT Silom |
| Chinatown (Yaowarat) | Loud, delicious | Street food, gold shops, Wat Traimit | MRT Wat Mangkon |
| Old City (Rattanakosin) | Historic, temple-dense | Grand Palace, Wat Pho, Wat Arun | Chao Phraya Express Boat |
| Thonglor/Ekkamai | Trendy, upscale | Craft cocktails, Japanese food, nightlife | BTS Thong Lo, Ekkamai |
| Chatuchak | Market, local | Weekend market shopping, parks | BTS Mo Chit, MRT Chatuchak |
| Ari | Hip, residential | Cafes, brunch, local Thai food | BTS Ari |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jay Fai (เจ๊ไฟ) | Street food (Michelin) | Old City | $$$ | The legendary street cook with goggles. Michelin-starred street food. The crab omelette is worth every baht. | Crab omelette, drunken noodles |
| Yaowarat Road Food Stalls | Street food | Chinatown | $ | The entire street becomes a food market after dark. Follow the smoke and the crowds. | Pad thai, grilled seafood, mango sticky rice |
| Thip Samai (ทิพย์สมัย) | Pad Thai | Old City | $ | Bangkok's most famous pad thai. The version wrapped in egg is their signature. Lines form early. | Superb pad thai (egg-wrapped) |
| Som Tam Nua | Thai | Siam | $ | Spicy papaya salad and fried chicken that packs heat. Locals and tourists queue together. | Som tam (papaya salad), fried chicken |
| Gaggan Anand | Fine dining | Sukhumvit | $$$ | Progressive Indian cuisine, repeatedly named Asia's best restaurant. 25-course emoji menu. Book months ahead. | Tasting menu |
| Raan Jay Fai's Crab Omelette | Street food | Old City | $$$ | The single dish that put Thai street food on the Michelin map. Wok-fried crab in a crispy egg blanket. | Crab omelette |
| Or Tor Kor Market | Market | Chatuchak | $-$$ | Thailand's freshest produce market. Tropical fruits you've never seen, ready-to-eat curries, clean and organized. | Mango sticky rice, fresh durian, nam prik sets |
| Supanniga Eating Room | Thai | Thonglor | $$ | Elevated traditional Thai recipes from eastern Thailand. Beautiful setting, incredible flavors. | Mieng kham, river prawn tom yum |
| Pee Aor Tom Yum Goong | Noodles | Saphan Khwai | $ | Creamy tom yum noodle soup that locals swear by. Rich, spicy, swimming with shrimp. | Tom yum goong noodles (creamy style) |
| After You Dessert Cafe | Dessert | Multiple | $$ | Thai dessert chain famous for Shibuya Honey Toast. Perfect sweet ending after spicy street food. | Shibuya Honey Toast, kakigori (shaved ice) |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Grand Palace (พระบรมมหาราชวัง) | Culture | Old City | $$ | 2-3 hours | The dazzling royal palace complex. Dress modestly (long pants, covered shoulders). Go early to beat crowds and heat. |
| Wat Pho (วัดโพธิ์) | Culture | Old City | $ | 1-2 hours | Home of the massive Reclining Buddha (46 meters long). Also famous for traditional Thai massage school. |
| Wat Arun (วัดอรุณ) | Culture | Thonburi | $ | 1 hour | The Temple of Dawn across the river. Climb the steep stairs for views. Most photogenic at sunset from the opposite bank. |
| Chatuchak Weekend Market (จตุจักร) | Shopping | Chatuchak | Free | 3-4 hours | Over 15,000 stalls on weekends. Clothes, antiques, pets, food. Bring cash, wear comfy shoes, stay hydrated. |
| Chao Phraya River Boat Tour | Scenic | Central | $-$$ | 2-3 hours | Take the public express boat for a cheap river tour past temples and skyscrapers. Much cheaper than tourist boats. |
| Jim Thompson House | Culture | Siam | $$ | 1-2 hours | Beautiful teak house museum of the American silk entrepreneur who mysteriously vanished. Lush garden setting. |
| Lumphini Park (สวนลุมพินี) | Nature | Silom | Free | 1-2 hours | Bangkok's Central Park. Monitor lizards roam freely. Great for morning tai chi or an evening jog. |
| Sky Bar at Lebua (State Tower) | Views | Silom | $$$ | 1 hour | The rooftop bar from "The Hangover Part II." 63 floors up with panoramic city views. Dress code enforced. |
| Maeklong Railway Market | Day trip | Samut Songkhram | $ | Half day | A market built on active train tracks. Vendors fold their stalls as the train passes through. 1 hour from Bangkok. |
| Floating Markets (Damnoen Saduak or Amphawa) | Day trip | Outside Bangkok | $-$$ | Half day | Boat through canal markets. Amphawa is more authentic and less touristy than Damnoen Saduak. Go on weekends. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sky Bar at Lebua | Rooftop bar | Silom | $$$ | The most famous rooftop bar in Bangkok. Dress smart, arrive for sunset, order the Hangovertini. |
| Khao San Road | Bars/backpacker | Old City | $ | Buckets of cocktails, street food at 2 AM, fire shows. Chaotic, sweaty, fun if you embrace it. |
| Thonglor Bar Scene | Cocktail bars | Thonglor | $$ | Bangkok's trendy cocktail strip. Iron Balls, Rabbit Hole, and 72 Courtyard complex. |
| Route 66 | Club | Rama 9 | $$ | Massive multi-room club playing Thai pop, EDM, and hip hop. Where Bangkok locals party. |
| Maggie Choo's | Speakeasy | Silom | $$-$$$ | Hidden underground bar beneath a hotel. Opium-den aesthetic, live jazz, theatrical cocktails. |

## Transit tips

- **BTS Skytrain**: Elevated train covering Sukhumvit and Silom lines. Clean, air-conditioned, fast. Buy a Rabbit card for convenience.
- **MRT**: Underground metro connecting Chatuchak, Chinatown, and Central Station. Expanding rapidly.
- **Chao Phraya Express Boat**: River transport connecting Old City temples to modern Sathorn. Cheap and scenic.
- **Tuk-tuks**: Fun but always negotiate price first. Never accept a "free" tuk-tuk ride (it's a shopping scam).
- **Grab**: Southeast Asia's Uber. Safer and cheaper than haggling with taxis. Use for everything.
- **Pro tip**: Bangkok traffic is legendary. Use BTS/MRT during rush hour or you'll sit in a cab for hours.
- **Motorcycle taxis**: Orange-vested riders who weave through traffic. Fast but terrifying. Good for short distances.
- **Songthaews**: Red truck-buses in Chiang Mai. Wave them down, hop in the back, pay when you get off.

## Useful Thai phrases

| English | Thai | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | สวัสดี | sawadee (+ kha/khrap) |
| Thank you | ขอบคุณ | khop khun (+ kha/khrap) |
| How much? | เท่าไหร่ | thao rai? |
| Delicious! | อร่อย! | aroy! |
| Too spicy | เผ็ดมาก | phet maak |
| Not spicy please | ไม่เผ็ด | mai phet |
| The bill please | เช็คบิลด์ | check bin |
| No thank you | ไม่เอา | mai ao |
